---
title: "Week 2"
author: Chunjian Zhang
format: 
  html:
    theme: cerulean
  pdf: default
---

# Introduction

In this tutorial we are looking at the Avian Influenza cases detected globally since 1997.

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r load-libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(rmarkdown)
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

[US CDC](https://www.cdc.gov/bird-flu/php/avian-flu-summary/chart-epi-curve-ah5n1.html)

## Reading Avian Influenza Data

```{r Reading-data, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
dat <- read_csv("avian_influenza_numbers.csv")
```

```{r head-data}
head(dat)
```

```{r head-data-10}
head(dat, 10)
```

```{r glimpse-data}
glimpse(dat)
```

```{r str-data}
str(dat)
```

This avian influenza dataset contains `r nrow(dat)` rows of records and `r ncol(dat)` columns of variables.

### Why is it important to know the dimension of your dataset?

Knowing this dataset dimensions helps assess data completeness, plan memory usage, and identify potential data entry errors.

### What are the variable names in the dataset?

```{r var-names}
names(dat)
```

### Variable Explanations
- `date`: Temporal stamp of avian influenza case documentation, employed for longitudinal trend analysis.
- `cases`: Quantified count of confirmed avian influenza cases, representing the epidemic magnitude at discrete time points.

```{r data-cleaned, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
data_cleaned <- dat |>
  select(Month, Australia, Egypt, `United States`)  
```

```{r inspect-vars}
glimpse(data_cleaned)
```
- **`Month`**: `<chr>` (character/string type), storing dates in the format `"1/1/1997"`, `"2/1/1997"`, etc.
- **`Australia`**: `<dbl>` (double/numeric type), storing the count of avian influenza cases in Australia (all values are `0` in the preview).
- **`Egypt`**: `<dbl>` (double/numeric type), storing the count of avian influenza cases in Egypt (all values are `0` in the preview).
- **`United States`**: `<dbl>` (double/numeric type), storing the count of avian influenza cases in the United States (all values are `0` in the preview).

---

- ✅ **Case count variables (`Australia`, `Egypt`, `United States`)**: Correct. They are numeric (`<dbl>`), which is appropriate for counting cases and performing statistical calculations.
- ⚠️ **`Month` variable**: Incorrect as currently stored. It is a `<chr>` (text) type, but it represents date information. It should be converted to a `<date>` type using `lubridate::mdy()` to enable time-series operations (e.g., sorting, filtering by year/month).

```{r convert-date}
library(lubridate)
data_monthly <- data_cleaned |>
    mutate(monthdate = lubridate::mdy(Month))

data_monthly %>% filter(is.na(monthdate))
```
```{r remove-invalid}
data_monthly <- data_monthly |> filter(!is.na(monthdate))
```

```{r check-dim}
# Dimensions of the cleaned dataset
dim(data_monthly)
# Dimensions of the original dataset (replace "dat" with your raw data object)
dim(data_cleaned)
```
We have lost 32 cases for which we did not have information about age or gender.

```{r summary-table}
data_monthly |>
    select(Egypt, Australia, `United States`) |>
    summary() |>
    kable(caption = "COVID-19 Age Summary")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
data_monthly |>
    ggplot(aes(x=Egypt)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +
    labs(x="Case counts in Egypt", y="")
```

```{r}
data_monthly |>
    # Drop the old month column
    select(!Month) |>
    # Pivot everything except monthdate
    pivot_longer(!monthdate) |>
    ggplot(aes(x=value, fill = name)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +
    labs(x="Case counts of Avian Influenza", y="")
```

```{r}
data_monthly |>
    # Drop the old month column
    select(!Month) |>
    # Pivot everything except monthdate
    pivot_longer(!monthdate) |>
    ggplot(aes(x=monthdate, y = value, fill = name)) +
    geom_col() +
    facet_wrap(~name, scales="free") +
    labs(x="Case counts of Avian Influenza", y="")
```
